metadata.title:
UNION DANCE STOPPED


BY POLICE YESTERDAY

pages.raw_text:
There must be no more public
dances in the city for personal
gain, but when conducted under
the management of some lodge or
organization it will not be
molested.


Following these instructions
from Daggett, Chief Waller issued
permit on Saturday to the Cen
trail Labor Union and the dancing
was we'll under way when Sergeant
McPhee, Detectives Weir and Mac
donald arrived and stopped it.


This actions of the socalled union
man, Funk, has caused unionists to
openly charge Funk with being a
tool in the hands Of the Washing
ton Water Power company.
j. 4. Conner, an employe Of the
city, openly charges Funk with be-
ing a personal representative of
the Washington Water Power com
pany. Conner owes his job to
Funk's influence, and on account of
his bold assertion will in all prob
ability lose 1t.


A campaign started among union
men this morning to force the clos
ing of the attractions at Natatorium
park owned by the Washington
Water Power company. They say
the roller skating rink runs there
at full btst on Sundays and is
much mofe noisy than the dancing
held at Mantle park. Efforts will
be made to stop this and all other
Sunday amusements out there.
